growth_records.strain: not specified (in vivo mouse infection model; background strain likely CMCP6)
growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.medium: in vivo - iron-overloaded (iron-dextran-treated) mouse host, not a defined lab medium
growth_records.cite: Kim et al. Integrative genome-scale metabolic analysis of Vibrio vulnificus for drug targeting and discovery. Molecular Systems Biology (2011). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.115. [PMC3049409]
growth_records.snippet: V. vulnificus, which proliferates inside iron-overloaded mice with an estimated in vivo doubling time of 72 min (Kim et al, 2003; Lee et al, 2007)
growth_records.notes: This paper (PMC3049409) cites a doubling time of 72 min (=1.2 h) for V. vulnificus growing in vivo in iron-overloaded mice, reported in earlier work (Kim et al 2003; Lee et al 2007), not measured in this study itself. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/1.2 h = 0.578 h^-1 using only the 72-min doubling time explicitly stated in this paper's text. No medium composition, temperature, or pH given (in vivo host environment, undefined). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

growth_records.strain: ENV1 (E-genotype environmental isolate, oyster, Louisiana; cluster 2 / lineage 2)

sec.units: mM (accumulated concentration in CFS after 24 h; NOT normalized to biomass or time)
sec.method: gas chromatography of short-chain fatty acids

growth_records.cite: Rasheedkhan Regina, Viduthalai et al. Loss of the Acetate Switch in Vibrio vulnificus Enhances Predation Defense against Tetrahymena pyriformis. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01665-21. [PMC8788688]
growth_records.snippet: High concentrations of acetate (up to 4 mM) were detected in the CFS ... short-chain fatty acids (acetate, propionate, butyrate, and valerate) in the CFS of ENV1 after 24 h of growth under aerobic conditions at RT
growth_records.notes: Qualitative/semi-quantitative overflow-metabolism study. Only measurement extractable is accumulated acetate concentration up to ~4 mM in cell-free supernatant after 24 h aerobic growth in 0.5x VNSS (measured by GC). No biomass (gDW), no growth rate, and no time-course are given, so a specific secretion rate (mmol/gDW/h) cannot be derived. Reported as concentration, not a rate; is_experimental:true but low confidence. Propionate/butyrate/valerate also assayed but not quantified in text.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 17277 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/17277

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
